risks: No managed identity or RBAC configuration — resources may default to key-based authentication, violating least-privilege principles
No monitoring or diagnostics resources (Log Analytics workspace, diagnostic settings) — operational blind spots in production
Cosmos DB serverless is single-region only with no disaster recovery or geo-redundancy
IoT Edge AI module deployment is a post-provisioning manual step not captured in IaC
SMS connector (Twilio or Azure Communication Services) requires separate external account setup after deployment
All resources use public network access with no private endpoints — larger attack surface
Storage account local auth (key access) not explicitly disabled per Azure security best practices
correctionsRecommended: Add managed identity configuration for service-to-service authentication (Stream Analytics to IoT Hub, Cosmos DB, and Service Bus)
Add a Log Analytics workspace resource and enable diagnostic settings on all resources for monitoring and alerting
Add Azure Key Vault for centralized secrets management
Explicitly disable local auth (key access) on the storage account (set allowSharedKeyAccess to false)
Disable key-based authentication on Cosmos DB and configure data-plane RBAC
Add resource tags across all resources for cost tracking and operational management
Document post-provisioning steps for IoT Edge AI module deployment manifest and container image configuration
Add explicit Cosmos DB database and container sub-resources for the anomaly event store
